scenarios.description: clone() on an empty Document returns a deep copy with distinct instance and cloned outputSettings and parser
scenarios.precondition: Document original = new Document("http://example.com"); // no child nodes
scenarios.expectedResult: clone != original; clone.location().equals(original.location()); clone.outputSettings() != original.outputSettings(); clone.parser() != original.parser(); clone.childNodes().isEmpty()
pseudocode: assert clone != original; assert clone.location().equals(original.location()); assert clone.outputSettings() != original.outputSettings(); assert clone.parser() != original.parser(); assert clone.childNodes().isEmpty();

scenarios.description: clone() on a shell Document preserves child structure and produces distinct child instances
scenarios.precondition: Document original = Document.createShell("http://site"); // has html, head, body
scenarios.expectedResult: clone.childNodes().size() == original.childNodes().size(); for each index i: clone.childNodes().get(i) != original.childNodes().get(i); clone.select("html").first() != original.select("html").first()
pseudocode: assert clone.childNodes().size() == original.childNodes().size(); for each i: assert clone.childNodes().get(i) != original.childNodes().get(i);

scenarios.description: clone() produces isolation: modifications to clone do not affect original
